figures.caption: Fig. 1: User taking a drink using our system. After selecting the mug with eye-tracking and selecting the action with EEG, the robot brings the mug close to the user’s face.
Fig. 2: Modular system architecture for our multimodal AR BRI.

Fig. 4: Experimental task setups. The three multi-step manipulation tasks performed in our experiments are illustrated. (Left) Drinking Task: the user drinks from the mug and then places it in the rack. (Center) Drawer Task: the user opens the drawer, places the spoon inside, and closes it. (Right) Oven Task: the user opens the oven, inserts the plate, and closes it. The figure also illustrates the sequence of actions required for each step, indicating the order of object and action (“Place” or “Use”) selections for the robot commands to be executed.
